title: Reading & Changing Code with AI
goal: Safely read, understand, and edit your product's code using AI.

blocks.html: You don't need to be a developer to understand what a file does — you need to ask AI well. It's great at explaining code in plain English, step by step.

blocks.question: You open a file full of code you don't understand. What's the safest first move with AI?

options.text: Paste it in and ask: 'Explain what this file does in plain English, step by step.'
options.explain: Right. Understand it first — then you can decide what, if anything, to change.

options.text: Ask it to rewrite the whole thing to be 'better'.
options.explain: You don't know what it does yet. Rewriting blind is how working things break.

options.text: Delete the parts you don't recognize.
options.explain: Never delete code you don't understand — those parts are usually load-bearing.

options.text: Ask it to add ten new features at once.
options.explain: Understand first, change later, and only one thing at a time.

lessons.title: Ask AI to Explain a File

blocks.html: Build the prompt you'd use to understand a scary file. Fill it in and save it.

blocks.title: Explain-a-file prompt
blocks.template: Explain this {what} in plain English for {who}. Focus on {focus}. Walk through it step by step, and flag anything risky.

fields.label: What is it
fields.placeholder: a Python file that handles payments

fields.label: Focus on
fields.placeholder: what could break if I change it

blocks.html: You want AI to change one line — a button color — in your app. Pick your approach and see how it plays out.

start.text: What's the safest way to make that one small change?

choices.label: Ask AI for the exact change, apply just that, and test it
Let an agent rewrite the whole file to 'modernize' it
Change lots of things at once to save time

safe.text: You ask for the exact one-line change, apply it, and test that one thing.
safe.outcome: This is the way. One small, specific change you can test in isolation keeps you safe — if it breaks, you know exactly what caused it and can undo it.

rewrite.text: You let an agent rewrite the whole file.
rewrite.outcome: Dangerous. A full rewrite can silently break things that were working fine. Small, testable changes beat big blind ones every time.

many.text: You change many things at once.
many.outcome: Risky. When something breaks, you won't know which change did it. One change at a time.

blocks.html: AI wrote you a change. Before you keep it, run this check — every time.

items.text: I read what changed and understand it in plain English.
I tested the one thing it was supposed to fix.
I checked it didn't break anything nearby.
I can undo it if something goes wrong.
